title: www_justice_gov_uk_courts_procedure_rules_civil_contents_frontmatter_Notes22_9b3a5c04d9.html
content: Notes to Accompany February 2001 22nd Supplement – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter Notes to Accompany February 2001 22nd Supplement Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » Notes to Accompany February 2001 22nd Supplement Menu ≡ Notes to Accompany February 2001 22nd Supplement Amendments to existing material are included as follows: Rules Part 6 rule 6.24(1)(ii)(Method of Service – General Provisions) substitute rule 6.27 for rule 6.26 Part 8 delete rule 8.2A (1) and (2) and insert new (1) and (2) (Issue of claim form without naming defendants) Part 10 add new sentence in brackets at end of rule 10.5 (Contents of Acknowledgement of Service) Part 17 delete rule 17.4(1)(a)(iii) and (iv) and insert new (iii). delete Rule 19.4 and substitute Rule 19.5 in wording in brackets at end of 17.4 (Amendments to statements of case after the end of a relevant limitation period) Part 19 delete rule 19.5(1)(c) and (d) and substitute new (c) (Special provisions about adding or substituting parties after the end of a relevant limitation period) insert new rule 19.5A (Special rules about parties in claims for wrongful interference with goods) insert new rule 19.8A (Power to make judgements binding on non-parties) Part 32 rule 32.13(1) delete wording after word ‘inspection’ and substitute new wording (Availability of witness statements for inspection) Part 40 insert new rule 40.20 with heading ‘III Declaratory Judgements’ Part 43 rule 43.2(1)(k)(i) insert additional wording after the word ‘agreement’ Amend wording in brackets following 43.2(1)(o) (Definitions and application) Part 44 rule 44.16 add additional wording to wording in brackets) (Adjournment where legal representative seeks to challenge disallowance of any amount of percentage increase) Part 48 rule 48.8 delete (1) and substitute new (1), (3) and (4) (Basis of detailed assessment of solicitor and client costs) Delete rule 48.9 (Conditional fees) RSC Order 15 delete rule 13A (Notice of claim to non-parties) and rule 16 (Declaratory judgment) RSC Order 79 rule 9 amend wording in paras (6)(b), (8)(b) and (10)(b) (Bail) RSC Order 95 rule 2 insert additional wording in para (3) (Entry of Satisfaction) RSC Order 109 rule 4 amend wording in para (5) (Release of Appellant on bail by the Court of Appeal) RSC Order 112 amend heading ‘Application for use of blood tests in determining paternity’ Amend wording in rule1, rule 2(1), rule 3(b), rule 4, rule 5, and rule 6 RSC Order 116 amend wording in rule 10 para 14(a) (Determination of the application) CCR Order 27 amend wording in rule 4 para (1)(b) (Mode of Applying) amend wording in rule 10 para (3) (Contents and service of order) CCR Order 47 amend wording in rule 5 paras (1), (2). (3), (4)(b), (5) and (6) (Family Law Reform Act 1969) CCR Order 49 rule 1 para (7) delete wording after word ‘judge’ (Access to Neighbouring Land Act 1992 Practice Directions Part 2B insert new para 15 (Race Relations) para 10.1 reference to para 9.13 should be to para 8.13 para 12 reference to para 8 should be to para 7 Part 3 para 7.10 reference should be to para 7.9 not para Part 5 para 5.5 reference should be to para 5.6 below not para 5.5 below para 5.6(2) reference should be to para 5.6(8)(a) not para 5.5(8)(a) Part 8B Table 1 reference should be to RSC O 106 r8 not RSC O 106 r8(1) para B14 reference in explanatory note should be to para 10.1(1) not para 10(1) para B14 in explanatory note reference should be Case Management preliminary stage practice direction and not Case Management preliminary stages practice direction Part 16 insert new para 10.3 after para 10.2 (General) Part 18 para 5.8(2) reference should be to rule 44.13(1) and not 43.5(5) Part 23 insert new paras 11A.1, 11A.2, 11A.3 and 11A.4 (Applications to stay claim where related criminal proceedings) after para 11.2 Part 25 para 1.1 Footnote 1 reference to Rule 25.1(1)(g) should be to Rule 25.1(1)(h) Part 31 para 4.4 reference to rule 31 should be to part 31 Part 32 para 1.4 delete Footnote 6 Part 35 para 2 reference to part 35.9 should be to rule 35.9 Part 36 para 7.7(3) reference to paras 6.5 and 6.6 should be to paras 5.5 and 5.6 Part 37 para 3.3 reference to para 8.8 should be to para 7.8 Part 39 para 1.4a reference should be to European Convention on Human Rights and not European Convention for Human Rights Part 39B delete para 2 Para 3.3(2) reference to ‘or real urgency’ should be to ‘of real urgency’ Part 40 para 9.1 reference to RSC 44 should be to RSC Order 44 para 11 reference to RSC 44 should be to RSC Order 44 Para 12 reference to RSC 44 should be to RSC Order 44 Part 40B para 9.2 reference to paragraph 8.3 should be to paragraph 9.3 Para 11.2 reference to rule 43.5(5) should be to rule 44.13(1) Part 48 para 50.4(2) reference to ‘on the basis that is has been’ should be to ‘on the basis that it has been’ Part 49 para 16.4 reference to CPR 3.2(e) should be to CPR 3.1(2)(e) Part 49B insert additional wording after ‘Companies Act 1985’ para 1(1) (General) Part 49F para 1.11 reference to Rule CPR rule 26.8 should be to rule 26.8 Part 49G para 2.1 reference to paragraph 22.2 should be to paragraph 2.2 Para 34.4(2) reference to paragraph 3(1)(c)(i) of the said rule 3 should be to paragraph (1)(c)(i) of the said rule 3 Part 52 add additional wording to title Appeals from Social Security Commissioners insert additional wording after word ‘under’ in para 21.5 insert additional wording at the end of para 22.3(2) (Appeal where court’s decision is final) para 22.8(3)(a) reference to para (3)(b)(I) should be to para 2(b)(i) para 23.4(1)(b)(i) reference to ‘that the defendant’ should be to ‘than the defendant’ table following para 20.3 reference to ‘Agriculture Land Tribunal’ should be to ‘Agricultural Land Tribunal’ and ‘Pension Act 1995’ should be ‘Pensions Act 1995’ Other Practice Directions Insolvency Proceedings add new wording at end of para 1.1(1) and insert new para 1.1(6) (Part One – General) para 13.4 reference to ‘affadavit’ should be to ‘affidavit’ Directors Disqualification amended to take account of the Limited Liability Partnerships Regulations 2001 and the Insolvency Act 2000 Protocols Practice Direction General para 1.2 reference to the words ‘specified in the Schedule to this Practice Direction’ should be replaced with the words ‘set out in para 5.1’ Forms N210 form and corresponding notes for defendant Form) N208C N208C revised ) EX80A form previously in former Schedule of Costs Forms but now reproduced in Forms section Coming into Force Rules The following come into force on 26 March 2001: Amendments to rules 6.24, 8.2A, 17.4, 19.5, 32.13, 43.2, 44.16, 48.8, 48.9 and Part 10 New rules 19.5A, 19.8A and 40.20 The omission of rule 48.9 and RSC Order 15, rules 13A and 16 The following come into force on the date of entry into force of s. 90 of the Access to Justice Act 1999 (anticipated to be 1 April 2001): Amendments to RSC Orders 79, 109 and 116 and to CCR Order 27 The following come into force on the date of entry into force of s. 23 of the Family Law Reform Act 1987 (anticipated to be 2 April 2001): Amendments to RSC Order 112 and CCR Order 47 Practice directions The following come into force on the date of entry into force of s.1 of the Race Relations (Amendment) Act 2000 (anticipated to be 2 April 2001): Amendment to PD 2B The following come into force on the date of entry into force of s. 6 of the Insolvency Act 2000 (anticipated to be 2 April 2001): Amendments to Directors Disqualification Proceedings Practice Direction so far as they relate to disqualification undertakings The following come into force on the date of entry into force of the Limited Liability Partnerships Regulations 2001 (anticipated to be 6 April 2001): Amendments to Insolvency Proceedings and Applications under the Companies Act 1985 and Insurance Companies Act 1982 Practice Directions Amendments to Directors Disqualification Proceedings Practice Direction so far as they relate to limited liability partnerships Forms Changes to Forms N210, N208C and EX80A come into force on 19 March 2001 All other amendments come in to force on 26 March 2001 Updated: Monday, 30 January 2017 Top ↑ Home Courts Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter Notes to
